It seems that the poster has also encountered the common problem with AMD's new generation, where the CPU also runs at full load when the graphics card is fully loaded. Even when the external graphics card is turned off and the integrated graphics are used, the CPU frequency drops directly. Additionally, it is recommended not to use sleep mode; when the laptop is unplugged, it's better to shut down after a period of non-use, because if sleep fails, it usually cannot be recovered within 2 hours, and this issue has been tested by many experts on foreign forums and found to be unsolvable. Currently, the best way to handle this is to turn off the small cores and reduce the frequency when you're out, which is similar to the situation with Intel's I9. When unplugged, just use energy-saving mode to turn off the small cores. I can't help but feel like spitting at AMD, their software for both graphics cards and CPUs is really terrible.

Unlike some motherboards (case 1) m2_1 PCIe 5.0 m2_2 PCIe 4.0 or (case 2) m2_1 PCIe 4.0 m2_2 PCIe 3.0 (case 3) m2_1 PCIe 4.0 x4 m2_2 PCIe 4.0 x2, these configurations would require installing a faster M.2 SSD into the m2_1 slot. Of course, this is only necessary if (case 1) you have bought a PCIe 5.0 SSD and need to plug it into a PCIe 5.0 slot (case 2) a PCIe 4.0 SSD plugged into a PCIe 3.0 slot, which indeed scores slower. M.2 SSDs typically operate at read/write speeds below 1000 MB/s in daily use (mechanical hard drives theoretically max out at 250 MB/s). Unless there's a high demand for fast read/write operations, such as transcoding, benchmarking, or speed testing, there shouldn't be much difference in everyday usage.

This performance is normal for a 61WHr battery carrying 400nit OLED and Intel CPU, these two major power consumers. OLEDs inherently don't save power compared to LCDs, and Lenovo laptops prioritize performance release over power saving. Their BIOSEC strategy is more aggressive, providing higher wattage even during idle or full load, so they can't compare with GRAM in terms of power efficiency. To save power, you can only lower brightness, enable dark mode, and turn off direct GPU connection. Unnecessary apps should also be removed.

Quadruplet.Opinion: don't save power compared to LCDs
can't compare with GRAM in terms of power efficiency
